sectionsUnavailable: A calibrated absolute-risk model with age, sex, ancestry, family history, and validation metadata is not supplied.
Symptoms, cognition, biomarkers, clinician review, and family-history context are not supplied.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The supplied derived APOE evidence reports an e3/e4 allele context. This is risk-factor context only, not a diagnosis.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: One APOE e4 copy can be associated with increased late-onset Alzheimer's disease risk, but it does not mean Alzheimer's disease is present or will definitely happen.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived CT call is available for rs429358, one of the APOE markers used for allele context in this package.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker is used as part of the derived APOE allele context and is not interpreted alone as a disease result.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived CC call is available for rs7412, one of the APOE markers used for allele context in this package.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker is used with rs429358 to describe APOE allele context and is not interpreted alone as a diagnosis.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The local fixture does not include a calibrated absolute-risk model, ancestry applicability metadata, age, sex, family history, biomarkers, or authenticated sample-report rows.
resultRows.sourceLabel: FDA Direct-to-Consumer Tests; NHGRI overview of polygenic risk scores
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can describe APOE context, but it cannot estimate a personal lifetime risk or replace clinician or genetic-counselor review.

resultRows.item: Public ApoE DNA Wellness sample sections
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample PDF shows report-summary, top-suggestion, ApoE allele-background, SNP-table, risk-narrative, and recommendation-block sections. These rows are used as output-format evidence only.
resultRows.sourceLabel: SelfDecode ApoE DNA Wellness sample report
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The sample PDF helps preserve the formal report structure, but its sample genotype, suggestions, and risk wording are not local findings or personal medical advice.

sampleRows.description: Rendered sample page 2 shows a Report Summary table with trait 'Your ApoE Genotype', gene ApoE, and an ApoE-e3/e3 sample genotype.
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report summary table reports an ApoE-e3/e3 genotype and explains that the table includes ApoE genotype information.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from rendered page 2 of the public ApoE sample PDF; this is observed sample output structure, not a local APOE genotype.

sampleRows.description: Rendered sample page 2 shows ranked suggestion headings and examples, with the sample watermark partially covering the first item.
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible top suggestions include maintaining healthy blood lipid levels, healthy body composition, omega-3 index, and high cognitive reserve; consuming a diverse plant-antioxidant diet; and reducing stress, toxic exposure, and oxidative stress.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from rendered page 2 of the public sample PDF as report-format evidence only; do not convert sample suggestions into local diet, supplement, or lifestyle advice.

sampleRows.description: Rendered pages 4-6 show the ApoE background section, common allele frequencies, possible genotype combinations, and the rs429358/rs7412 marker explanation.
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample introduction describes ApoE roles in lipid transport and brain biology, names common e2/e3/e4 alleles, and explains that rs429358 and rs7412 define amino-acid differences in ApoE.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from rendered sample pages 4-6 as observed explanatory report structure; local interpretation remains limited to supplied derived evidence.

sampleRows.description: Rendered sample page 11 shows columns for SNP, Your Genotype, Associations, and Reference, followed by the sample ApoE-e3/e3 genotype sentence.
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample genotype table reports rs429358 TT and rs7412 CC, with both rows citing reference 52, and then states the sample has an ApoE-e3/e3 genotype with average ApoE protein activity and levels.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from rendered page 11 of the public sample PDF; these sample genotypes are not used as local genotype evidence.

sampleRows.description: Rendered page 11 contains the risk narrative; the sample watermark obscures part of two lines, so only visible and OCR-supported wording is preserved.
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample states typical Alzheimer's disease risk with an approximately 9% lifetime-risk figure, average sleep-apnea and insomnia risk, and that the ApoE-e3/e3 genotype alone does not significantly modify heart-disease risk while other lifestyle factors may matter.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from rendered page 11 of the public sample PDF as observed sample output only; do not infer local absolute risk, dementia status, sleep status, or heart-disease risk.

sampleRows.description: Rendered page 11 shows the first recommendation heading and visible example food/compound bullets before the sample PDF ends.
sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The first visible recommendation says taking steps to reduce amyloid levels in the brain may help reduce Alzheimer's risk for the sample genotype and lists plant-based compounds/foods such as olive oil, turmeric, red wine, green tea, and rosemary.
sampleRows.item: Proactively reduce amyloid levels in the brain
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from rendered page 11 as report-format evidence only; do not turn this into local diet, alcohol, supplement, or treatment guidance.

riskContext.plainEnglishMeaning: APOE e4 is a risk factor for late-onset Alzheimer's disease. It is not a diagnosis, and many other genetic, health, family, lifestyle, and environmental factors can matter.
riskContext.professionalReview: A clinician or genetic counselor can help interpret APOE context alongside age, symptoms, family history, ancestry, and other health information.

references.title: FDA 510(k) K192073: Helix Genetic Health Risk App for Late-Onset Alzheimer's Disease
National Institute on Aging: Alzheimer's Disease Genetics Fact Sheet
NHGRI overview of polygenic risk scores
SelfDecode ApoE DNA Wellness sample report

genotypeSummary.observedValue: calibrated absolute-risk model, applicability metadata, symptoms, cognition, and clinician review unavailable
The public sample report uses an ApoE-e3/e3 sample genotype with rs429358 TT and rs7412 CC as report-format evidence.

missingInputs: Age, sex, family-history, symptom, biomarker, and clinician-confirmed context
Early-onset familial Alzheimer's disease gene assessment
uncertainty: No calibrated probability model is supplied for this local package.
APOE e4 status is only one part of late-onset Alzheimer's disease risk context.
Public sample report rows preserve output structure and citations but are not local findings.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The public sample PDF's genotype, risk statements, and suggestion language are output-format evidence only.
The package covers APOE allele context and does not assess APP, PSEN1, PSEN2, or other early-onset familial Alzheimer's disease genes.
The report does not diagnose disease, assess current cognition, or recommend medication, supplements, imaging, lab testing, or treatment changes.
alleleMappingContext: This fixture uses an already-derived APOE e3/e4 row plus rs429358 and rs7412 marker rows for validation.
If either APOE marker is missing or ambiguous, the local agent must mark APOE allele context unavailable instead of inferring it.
